name: Kuzu
description: Kuzu is an embedded property graph database built for speed and scalability. It implements the openCypher query language on a columnar storage engine, making it well-suited for analytical graph workloads on large datasets. Kuzu supports vector search and full-text search natively, runs in-process with zero external dependencies, and provides client APIs for C++, Python, Rust, JavaScript, and Java.
format_on_disk: Columnar Storage (multi-file: node/rel groups, WAL, catalog)
core_strengths: openCypher query language for expressive graph pattern matching
Columnar storage engine optimized for analytical graph queries
Built-in vector similarity search with HNSW index
Zero-configuration embedded deployment with no external daemon
Cross-language client APIs (C++, Python, Rust, JS, Java)
best_use_cases: Analytical graph queries over large property graph datasets
Graph-based recommendation and ranking (PageRank, community detection)
Fraud detection with complex relationship pattern matching
Knowledge graph traversal with Cypher path queries
Hybrid search combining graph patterns with vector similarity
remarks: Kuzu is designed for analytical graph workloads (read-heavy) rather than high-throughput transactional graph updates. The columnar storage format uses multiple files on disk by default, not a single file. Writes are serialized through a single-writer model; concurrent read access is supported. The primary implementation is C++; language clients wrap the C API.
